source: ISRO Science Data Archive - Chandrayaan-2 PRADAN
current_use: Primary download source for DFSAR, OHRC, and TMC-2 raw ZIP products used in the workspace.
judge_value: Shows the demo is built on official Chandrayaan-2 data rather than synthetic screenshots.
next_upgrade: Add direct product IDs and timestamps to every analysis card.

current_use: Mission-data context and payload documentation reference.
judge_value: Explains why DFSAR, OHRC, and TMC-2 are the right payload combination for the problem.
next_upgrade: Extract product metadata fields into a searchable evidence table.

current_use: Scientific criterion anchor: candidate subsurface ice should be evaluated with radar polarimetric logic, especially CPR > 1 and DOP < 0.13 where products support it.
judge_value: Connects the hackathon scoring model to a very recent ISRO/PRL science result.
next_upgrade: Compute exact CPR and DOP when complex/full polarimetric parameters are available, instead of the current intensity-ratio proxy.

current_use: AOI discovery and visual product selection route for south-pole TMC-2 and payload browsing.
judge_value: Shows the team can navigate real planetary-data catalog workflows.
next_upgrade: Capture a reproducible AOI search recipe in the UI.

source: VEDAS note on downloading Chandrayaan-2 OHRC products
current_use: Operational guidance for OHRC product discovery and download workflow.
judge_value: Supports the OHRC hazard-inspection part of the workflow.
next_upgrade: Use OHRC geometry CSVs for exact footprint overlay.

source: NASA Planetary Data System Geosciences Node / LRO LOLA
current_use: External validation reference for lunar topography and polar products; not yet downloaded because high-resolution products can be large.
judge_value: Demonstrates an independent validation path beyond Chandrayaan-2 data.
next_upgrade: Add a lightweight LOLA south-pole reference layer for cross-checking TMC-2 slopes.

current_use: Validation target and scientific rationale for the current low-sun illumination, shadow-persistence, and cold-trap proxy layers derived from the TMC-2 DTM.
judge_value: Upgrades the concept from image scoring to mission planning: ice evidence must be balanced with illumination and communications.
next_upgrade: Validate the proxy against ephemeris-based polar illumination products.

current_use: Reference atlas for PSRs, slope-map conventions, and south-pole cartographic framing.
judge_value: Gives judges recognizable planetary-science context for PSRs, slopes, and traverse planning.
next_upgrade: Use atlas PSR/feature names to label science targets.

current_use: Target production-grade processing stack for future PDS/planetary image pipeline hardening; not required for the current lightweight web demo.
judge_value: Shows the prototype has a path to serious planetary image processing beyond a hackathon MVP.
next_upgrade: Create an ISIS/QGIS processing branch for map-projected product validation.
